title: Savory Crunchy Filipino Lamb Leg Grilled Lumpia
description: A savory and crunchy twist on traditional Filipino lumpia, featuring marinated lamb leg and a grilled twist. Perfect for a weeknight dinner or meal prep.

ingredients.name: vegetable spring rolls wrappers
ingredients.notes: usually found in the freezer section of most grocery stores

instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together vegetable oil, onion, garlic, ginger, salt, and black pepper to make the marinade.
Add the lamb leg to the marinade and mix until coated. Cover and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight.
Preheat a grill or grill pan to medium-high heat. Remove the lamb leg from the marinade and cook for 4-5 minutes per side, or until cooked to your desired level of doneness.
Let the lamb leg rest for 10 minutes before slicing into thin strips.
In a large pan or wok, heat 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil over medium heat. Add the sliced lamb leg and cook until browned, about 3-4 minutes.
Meanwhile, prepare the lumpia filling by shredding the cooked lamb leg into small pieces.
Add the shredded lamb to the pan with the cooked onion and stir-fry until combined.
To assemble the lumpia, lay a spring roll wrapper on a flat surface with one corner pointing towards you. Place 1 tablespoon of the lamb filling in the center of the wrapper.

Brush the edges of the wrapper with water and fold the bottom half over the filling, then fold in the sides and roll up the wrapper to form a tight cylinder. Repeat with the remaining wrappers and filling.

Heat about 1-2 inches of vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. When the oil is hot, add a few of the lumpia and fry until golden brown and crispy, about 2-3 minutes per side. Drain on paper towels.
Serve the grilled lamb leg with the crispy lumpia and shredded iceberg lettuce on the side, garnished with crushed pork rinds.
To make the dish more substantial, serve with steamed rice or noodles on the side.
